title: sterigmostemum sulphureum subsp. sulphureum
intro: Sterigmostemum sulphureum subsp. sulphureum, also known as the Golden Spleenwort, is a species of fern native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which can be attributed to the presence of various compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The Golden Spleenwort has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the Golden Spleenwort can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various ways.
